meta.pageTitle: Timothy J. Owen — Solutions Engineering Resume
meta.notes: SE (Solutions/Sales Engineering) profile. Use timowen.me/se. This is a transition profile — Tim has no verified quota-carrying sales or SE title. Lead with technical communication/translation, not prior SE tenure. Tailor skillGroups order and experience bullets per role before generating docx. Do not import income projections, job-title rankings, or 'Mission Systems Portfolio' framing from the 2026-07-04 multi-LLM career-strategy brainstorm — that source is speculative, not verified against USER_PROFILE.md.

header.name: Timothy J. Owen, D.M.A.
header.subtitle: U.S. Army Veteran (20 Years) · Active Federal Secret Clearance · Cloud & Cybersecurity Solutions Engineering

objective: Retired 20-year U.S. Army veteran with an active federal Secret-level security clearance, moving into cloud and cybersecurity solutions engineering. Twenty years spent training new personnel, briefing senior leadership, and coordinating large-scale operations built the same skill a solutions engineer uses every day: translating complex technical material for the person in front of you. Completed two consecutive 16-week Washington Vets-to-Tech intensive programs (Server & Cloud Applications, Aug 2025; Cybersecurity, Dec 2025) and a government IT internship, earning CompTIA Network+, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Foundational C#, and PCEP certifications.

skillGroups.title: Technical Communication
items: Live technical instruction to mixed-preparation audiences
Briefing senior leadership under time pressure

transferableSkills.title: Technical Translation
transferableSkills.description: Instructed trainees, mid-level leaders, and senior leadership alike, adjusting explanations for widely differing levels of preparation, the same skill a solutions engineer applies to a customer's team.

transferableSkills.title: Automation
transferableSkills.description: Built Python tools (Subnet Calculator, VA Ratings Calculator, Tempo Finder) using algorithmic problem-solving relevant to technical demos and proof-of-concept work.

transferableSkills.title: Adaptability
transferableSkills.description: Sustained mission-critical operations across 20 years of military service and rapid IT upskilling, translatable to dynamic, fast-moving customer environments.

transferableSkills.title: Product Thinking
transferableSkills.description: Designed a spaced-repetition practice system grounded in cognitive science research, evidence of translating a domain problem into a technical solution.

bullets: Communicated technical requirements and solutions clearly to non-technical agency staff across account, software, and workstation issues.
Diagnosed and resolved three network connectivity failures by tracing physical connections through patch panels and verifying switch port assignments, in mixed supervised and independent capacity.
Operated within a strict state data privacy compliance framework across the full engagement.

Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science; 100% lab completion rate across security hardening, threat intelligence, and vulnerability assessment.
Hosted a Kali Linux virtual machine in VirtualBox for cybersecurity exploration exercises.
Completed 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science; 100% lab completion rate across Linux, Windows Server, networking, AWS, and Python scripting.
Built a multi-VM VirtualBox network with Active Directory; solo capstone provisioned a single VM using Vagrant with an instructor-template Ansible playbook.

experience.role: Production Manager / Library, Security & Operations Supervisor
bullets: Sustained 100% readiness across 400+ public events for audiences totaling 2.5M+, coordinating a large equipment inventory.
Delivered resilience and performance training to 200 personnel across 10 cohorts as a certified Level 2 facilitator.
Administered personnel security clearance records for 40+ team members.
Instructed 100+ new trainees and advised 20 mid-level and 40 senior leaders, assessing 400 student performances across audiences with widely differing levels of preparation.
Contributed as part of a curriculum team to a 58% reduction in training cycle time.
Achieved a 100% student pass rate against the program's full testing standard; awarded the Distinguished Leader Award, Army Leadership Development Program (2010).
Managed HR administration and library operations for 40+ personnel; deployed in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom (2007).

education.degree: Cybersecurity Certificate (18 credits, B.S. Computer Science)
Server & Cloud Applications Certificate (18 credits, B.S. Computer Science)

projects.title: SIP — Systematic Instrumental Practice
projects.description: Android-first Flutter application for serious instrumental musicians. Uses FSRS-5 spaced repetition scheduling, Python/FastAPI backend, and py-fsrs. Grounded in doctoral dissertation applying Edwin Gordon's Music Learning Theory. Multi-model AI design council produced canonical design specification v1.0. In active development.

projects.title: Vagrant + Ansible VM Provisioning (WaV2T Capstone)
projects.description: Solo capstone project: provisioned a single VM via Vagrant, using an Ansible playbook customized from an instructor-provided template.

projects.title: Subnet Calculator
projects.description: Python tool automating IPv4 subnet calculations for network troubleshooting and study.

projects.title: Tempo Finder
projects.description: Binary-search algorithm identifying optimal practice tempos (30–240 BPM) at O(log n) efficiency.
